text: Neurology appointment today. Dr. Patel says the tremor is 'mild to moderate.' It doesn't feel mild when I'm trying to eat soup.
Looked at the credit card statement. $14,208. I try not to think about it all at once.
PT session. Worked on balance and gait. The therapist says I'm doing well. I still grab the rail every time.
First Rock Steady Boxing class. Punched the bag for an hour. My arms are sore but my head feels clear.
Picked up the levodopa refill. $45 copay. That's one less thing to worry about this week.
Carmen called to check on me. I told her I'm fine. I'm mostly fine.
Part-time shift at Henderson & Briar. Filed papers for four hours. My hand cramped up twice but I got through it.
Financial counseling session at GreenPath. Maria laid out the numbers. It's bad but she says we can work with it.
Tried to text Luis back. Took me five minutes to type 'I'm ok. Love you.' He doesn't know how long it takes.
Made scrambled eggs without dropping the pan. Progress.
Boxing class. Coach paired me with Helen, who has tremor too. We laughed at our shaking gloves.
My right hand was steady for two hours this morning. I wrote a grocery list by hand. It felt normal.
PT session. Worked on balance. The therapist says I'm improving but I still grab the rail every time.
Work shift. The new phone system has tiny buttons. I asked them to switch me back to the old one.
Support group Saturday. A woman named Doris has had Parkinson's for eight years and still drives. That gave me hope.
OT session. Linda taught me grip exercises. She gave me a fat pen that's easier to hold. Small thing but it helps.
Luis called tonight. He asked if I need money. I said no. I do but I can't take it from him.
Walked to the mailbox and back. Three blocks. My legs felt heavy but I made it without stopping.
Couldn't open a jar of pickles. Used the rubber grip thing Linda gave me. It worked. I cried anyway.
Watching TV and my hand won't stop shaking. I put it under a pillow and pretend it's not happening.
Boxing class. We did footwork drills today. I'm slower than everyone but Coach says consistency matters more.
PT session. Balance is better. The therapist filmed my gait and showed me the improvement. I could see it.
GreenPath session about credit card consolidation. Maria found an option that saves $40/month in interest. Every bit counts.
Support group. We talked about telling people at work. I haven't told my boss yet. I should.
Neurology follow-up. Dr. Patel says stay the course. I wanted him to say I'm getting better. He said 'stable is good.'
Tried voice-to-text on my phone for the first time. It understood most of what I said. This changes things.
Bad tremor day. Spilled coffee on my work desk. My coworker cleaned it up without making a big deal. I'm grateful.
Carmen came Saturday to help with bills. We sorted everything into piles. She made it feel less scary.
Cooked dinner by myself. Simple pasta with sauce from a jar. Set a timer like the OT said. Nothing burned.
I miss being able to write. My handwriting looks like a child's now. I used to have beautiful cursive.
Boxing class. Six weeks in. My stamina is better. I can do the full warmup without sitting down now.
Work shift. Answered phones all morning. I'm good at phones. Nobody can see my hands on the phone.
GreenPath session about medical debt. We set up a $15/month plan with the neurology clinic. Feels manageable.
Pharmacy pickup. The pharmacist knows my name now. She puts the cap on loosely so I can open it.
Support group. A new person came today. He looked terrified. I remembered feeling that way. I said hello first.
OT session. Linda helped me set up a non-slip mat under the cutting board. Chopping is safer now.
Thinking about retirement. I have $47,000 saved. I'm 61. The math doesn't work and I know it.
Carmen took me to the farmers market. I picked out tomatoes. She carried the bags. We didn't talk about it.
Good day today. Steady hands until 3pm. Made lunch, did dishes, walked to the corner. Almost normal.
Church potluck. Brought store-bought cookies. Nobody cared. The pastor asked how I was doing. I said 'hanging in there.'
Work shift. Boss gave me a new task: organizing the digital files on the shared drive. I can do it slowly. No tremor needed for clicking.
Boxing class. Helen wasn't there today. I missed her. Coach said she had a bad week. I'll call her.
GreenPath retirement review. Maria showed me the gap between what I have and what I'll need. I stared at the number for a long time.
Support group. Topic was exercise. Everyone agreed boxing is the best thing. I felt proud I stuck with it.
Neurology follow-up. Dr. Patel noticed the tremor is a bit worse in my right hand. He added ropinirole. Low dose. We'll see.
Luis visited for the weekend. He cooked dinner both nights. We watched old movies and I fell asleep on the couch. Best weekend in months.
Started the new medication today. No side effects yet. Fingers crossed.
Can't stop thinking about what Dr. Patel said. 'Slight increase.' I know what direction this goes.
Carmen asked if I've thought about moving closer to her. I said no. This is my apartment. I'm not ready.
OT session. Voice exercises. Linda says my voice is getting softer and I don't notice. She's right. I'll practice.
Work shift. Four hours of filing. My hand locked up near the end but I finished. Sat in the car for ten minutes after.
PT session. Therapist added a new exercise for my hip. She says posture matters for Parkinson's. I believe her.
GreenPath budget adjustment. Maria trimmed $85/month by cutting subscriptions I forgot I had. I feel stupid and relieved.
Called Luis tonight. He wants me to try a video call next time. I told him the phone is hard enough.
Dropped a glass in the kitchen. It shattered. I stood there for a minute before I could pick up the pieces. Carmen would have helped.
The ropinirole makes me a little dizzy when I stand up. Dr. Patel said give it time. I'm trying.
Support group. Topic was staying active in the heat. Everyone complained about Austin summers. It felt normal.
Walked to CVS for the medication refill. It's six blocks. I timed myself. 22 minutes. Used to be 12.
Bad night. Couldn't sleep because my leg kept moving. The medication side effects are real.
Made breakfast for myself. Eggs and toast. Only dropped the butter knife once. Counting that as a win.
Work shift. Someone asked why my hand shakes. I said 'just a tremor.' They said 'oh.' That was the whole conversation.
Primary care checkup. Dr. Huang says blood pressure is good. Vitamin D is low. One more pill to take.
GreenPath debt check. Credit card balance down $500 in six months. At this rate I'll be debt-free by 2027. I'll be 64.
Support group. Topic was assistive technology. Someone showed a weighted pen. I ordered one on the spot. Luis helped me from his phone.
OT session. Linda set up voice-to-text on my phone properly and showed me how to use Siri for reminders. I feel like I learned something real today.
Neurology billing called about the $340 balance. They agreed to $15/month. Another payment plan. My life runs on payment plans.
Boxing class. We had a visitor today — a neurologist from UT who studies exercise and Parkinson's. She said what we're doing matters. I needed to hear that.
Carmen asked how I'm really doing. Not the 'I'm fine' version. I told her the truth. The tremor is harder. The money is tight. She just listened.
Tried to use the patient portal online. Couldn't type my password. Gave up and called the office instead.
Watched a cooking show tonight. Thought about all the recipes I can't make anymore. Then thought about the ones I still can.
Boxing class. Coach taught us a new combination. I got it on the fourth try. My brain is still sharp even if my hands aren't.
PT session. Balance test scores improved again. The therapist high-fived me. I almost missed her hand.
GreenPath retirement session. Maria talked about delaying Social Security until 67. Five more years of working. I don't know if I can.
Support group. Topic was caregiver relationships. I thought about Carmen and cried a little. The group understood.
Carmen took me for groceries and errands. She drove. I navigated. We got everything done in two hours. Good team.
Pharmacy pickup. The copay went up to $78 because of the ropinirole. I didn't say anything. Just paid.
My boss asked me if I'm 'doing okay' with a look. I know that look. I need to have the conversation soon.
Six months of regular PT. My balance is measurably better than February. The numbers don't lie even when my body feels unreliable.
Watched the sunset from my apartment window. My hand was steady for once. I held my coffee and just looked.
Tried to write a birthday card for Carmen. My handwriting was illegible. I typed it on the computer instead. It took 20 minutes.
Work shift. Good day. Filed everything, answered the phone, even made small talk with a client. Almost forgot about the tremor.
Boxing class. Helen came back after two weeks out. She looked tired. We punched bags next to each other. No words needed.
GreenPath 401k rollover session. Maria says I should move the old Henderson & Briar account to an IRA. I said okay but I don't fully understand it.
Neurology follow-up. Dr. Patel increased the ropinirole slightly. He looked at my tremor diary and said I'm 'managing well.' That word — managing.
Support group. Topic was fall prevention. Someone showed photos of their bathroom with grab bars. I should get those.
OT session. Linda worked on tremor strategies. Weighted wrist cuffs during meals. They help more than I expected.
Luis called. He asked again about moving to Houston to be closer. I love him for asking. The answer is still no.
Opened the mailbox and there were four medical bills. I put them on the pile and closed my eyes for a minute.
Made soup from scratch. Used the timer, the non-slip mat, the weighted spoon. All the tools working together. It tasted like home.
Had the conversation with my boss about the tremor. He was kind. He said we'll figure it out. I believe him.
Work shift. Boss moved me to a desk with a larger monitor and keyboard. It helps. Nobody made a fuss about it.
Pharmacy pickup. Both prescriptions ready. $78 total. The pharmacist suggested a pill organizer. I already have one.
Boxing class. Coach said my posture has improved a lot since February. I stood a little taller leaving class.
Support group. Holiday stress management topic. Everyone worried about the same things: family, money, energy. I'm not alone in this.
OT session. Tremor management focus. Linda taught me to anchor my elbow on the table when eating. Simple but effective.
Credit card statement: $14,195. Down from $14,208 in February. Thirteen dollars of real progress plus all the interest I paid. It's a slow fight.
Can't stop worrying about Christmas. Luis wants to come but gas is expensive for him too. I won't let him spend money he doesn't have.
Requested a home safety assessment through OT. Carmen helped me write the letter. She typed while I dictated.
Quiet Sunday. Made coffee. Read the newspaper. My hand cooperated until page three. I switched to holding the mug with my left.
Church service. The choir sang 'Amazing Grace.' I hummed along because my voice is too soft to sing. Nobody noticed.
Work shift. Last shift before the holidays. My coworkers gave me a card. They all signed it. I put it on my fridge.
GreenPath year-end review. Total debt down from $32K to $29.8K. Maria said that's real progress. I wanted it to be more.
PT year-end session. Balance improved. Gait steady. Therapist said to keep up the home exercises through the holidays.
Luis called to finalize Christmas plans. He's driving up the 21st. I told him don't bring gifts, just bring yourself. He said 'and tamales.'
Bad tremor day. Couldn't zip my jacket. Stood in the hallway for five minutes. Then I put on the pullover instead and went outside.
Carmen drove me to get groceries for Luis's visit. We bought his favorite cereal. She didn't say anything but I know she snuck extra things into my pantry.
Luis is here. Three days of not being alone. He fixed the leaky faucet and organized my kitchen drawers without being asked. I didn't cry until after he left.
Christmas Eve. Luis and I ate tamales and watched a movie. I fell asleep at 9pm. He put a blanket on me. I woke up feeling safe.
Luis left today. The apartment is quiet again. The cereal box on the counter is the only evidence he was here.
Walked around the block on New Year's Eve. Cold but clear. Made one resolution: keep going.
Neurology follow-up. Dr. Patel says tremor is slightly worse in cold weather. No medication change. Recheck in March.
Work shift. First one of the new year. My boss asked how the holidays were. I said 'quiet but good.' That was true.
GreenPath new year session. Maria set 2025 targets: credit card below $12K by December. Emergency fund goal: $500. I wrote it on a sticky note.
Boxing class. New year, same gloves. Three new people joined. One of them is only 45. Parkinson's doesn't care about age.
Support group. Someone brought their adult daughter. She asked questions about how to help without hovering. I thought about Luis and Carmen.
Tried to type an email to Luis. Got frustrated after three sentences. Switched to voice-to-text. He called me back in ten minutes.
Took my medication on time every day this week. Used the pill organizer and the alarm. The system works when I let it.
Lay in bed thinking about what I'll do if I can't work anymore. The numbers don't add up. I pushed it out of my mind and went to sleep.
Made arroz con pollo from my mother's recipe. Used the weighted spoon and the timer. It tasted right. I ate it slowly and remembered her kitchen.
One year since my first Rock Steady Boxing class. I can do fifteen more reps than when I started. My right hook is no joke.
Work shift. Phones and filing. Steady hands until noon, then the tremor kicked in. I switched to phone duty for the afternoon.
PT session. New exercises: single-leg stands and heel-to-toe walking. I wobbled but didn't fall. The therapist spotted me the whole time.
Boxing class. Sparred with Helen for the first time. She's stronger than she looks. We both laughed the whole time.
GreenPath credit card session. Maria says I could be credit card debt-free by early 2027 if I keep this pace. If.
Valentine's Day. Nobody to celebrate with. Carmen sent a card. Luis sent a text with hearts. I made myself a nice dinner and called it enough.
Tried the new large-print keyboard Linda recommended. It's ugly but I can actually see the letters. Typed a full email to Luis in under five minutes.
Woke up at 3am with my right arm rigid. Lay there waiting for it to loosen. Thought about calling Carmen. Didn't. It passed by 4am.
Support group. Topic was accepting help. A man named George said 'asking for help is its own kind of strength.' I wrote it on a napkin.
Went to church. Sat in my usual pew. The woman next to me held my hand during prayer. I don't think she noticed the tremor. Or maybe she did and didn't care.
The ropinirole seems to be helping more lately. Fewer 'bad hand' hours in the afternoon. I'll take whatever I can get.
Work shift. Organized the entire filing cabinet. Took me all four hours but it's done. My boss said it looks great.
OT session. Voice projection exercises. Linda had me read out loud from a book. I was louder than I thought I'd be. There's still volume in me.
Pharmacy pickup. Levodopa refill. $45. The pharmacist asked if I wanted automatic refills. I said yes. One less thing to remember.
Neurology follow-up. Dr. Patel added an afternoon medication booster. The morning dose wears off by 2pm. This should help the gap.
Support group. Topic was driving. Two people said they stopped. One said her family made her stop. I'm still driving but I grip the wheel so hard my knuckles turn white.
Carmen found a senior exercise class at the rec center. She wants me to go with her. I said maybe. I meant probably.
Credit card balance dropped below $14,000 for the first time. Twelve months of minimum payments and a few extra twenties. Slow but real.
Bad day. Dropped my phone, couldn't pick it up for a minute because my hand was shaking too much. A stranger picked it up and handed it to me. I said thank you three times.
Made coffee and sat on the porch. The mockingbird was back. We stared at each other. I think we have an understanding.
Thinking about the retirement shortfall meeting next month. I know the numbers are bad. But Maria always has a plan. That's why I keep going.
Boxing class. Spring session started. Coach introduced a balance beam exercise. I surprised myself. Steady all the way across.
Work shift. Good day. No tremor until the last hour. My boss said 'you're doing great.' I don't think he says that to everyone.
GreenPath retirement session. The projected gap is big. Maria suggested working until 66 and maxing out catch-up contributions. I nodded like I understood all of it.
PT session. Updated exercises for fall prevention. The therapist says my core is stronger than six months ago. My body is still fighting even when it doesn't feel like it.
Opened the insurance paperwork Carmen and I need to sort through Saturday. It's three inches thick. I closed it and made tea instead.
Carmen came to help with insurance paperwork. We sat at the kitchen table for two hours. She filled out the forms. I told her what to write. Our system.
Walked to the mailbox and back without my cane for the first time in a month. My legs were strong. The morning medication was at full strength. I stood at the corner and smiled.
Support group. A woman said 'we're not broken, we're adapting.' I've been thinking about that all day. Adapting. That's what this is.
Tonight I used voice-to-text to send Luis a long message about my week. Boxing, work, the mockingbird. He wrote back 'Mom you sound like you.' That was enough.
Sitting in my apartment after a full day. Medication wearing off. Tremor starting up. But the day was good. I made it count. That's the job.
